games.name: Yahtzee
games.equipment: 5 standard dice, scorecard per player, pencil
games.objective: Score points by rolling five dice to make specific combinations across 13 categories. The player with the highest total score wins.

setup.title: Gather equipment
setup.description: Each player needs a scorecard. You need 5 standard six-sided dice and a cup for rolling (optional but traditional).

setup.title: Determine turn order
setup.description: Each player rolls one die. The highest roller goes first. In case of a tie, those players re-roll.

setup.title: Understand the scorecard
setup.description: The scorecard has 13 scoring categories: 6 in the Upper Section (Aces through Sixes) and 7 in the Lower Section (Three of a Kind, Four of a Kind, Full House, Small Straight, Large Straight, Yahtzee, Chance).

gameplay.title: Roll all five dice
gameplay.description: On your turn, roll all five dice. This is your first roll. You must roll at least once per turn.

gameplay.title: Decide which to keep
gameplay.description: After the first roll, set aside any dice you want to keep. You can keep as many or as few as you likeέΑΦeven none.

gameplay.title: Roll remaining dice (2nd roll)
gameplay.description: Re-roll any dice you didn't keep. You now have up to one more re-roll remaining.

gameplay.title: Final re-roll (optional)
gameplay.description: Optionally re-roll any dice one last time for your third and final roll. You cannot roll more than 3 times total per turn.

gameplay.title: Score your dice
gameplay.description: After your final roll, you MUST mark a score in exactly one unfilled category on your scorecard. If no category fits, you must mark a zero in any unused category.

gameplay.title: Pass to next player
gameplay.description: Play passes clockwise. Each player takes turns until all 13 categories are filled. Then final scores are tallied.

scoring.name: Aces
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 1

scoring.name: Twos
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 2

scoring.name: Threes
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 3

scoring.name: Fours
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 4

scoring.name: Fives
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 5

scoring.name: Sixes
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 6

scoring.name: Upper Section Bonus
scoring.description: If your total for the Upper Section is 63 or more, you earn a bonus
scoring.example: Score 63+ in AcesέΑΥSixes to earn 35 extra points (equivalent to scoring 3 of each number)

scoring.name: Three of a Kind
scoring.description: At least three dice showing the same number

scoring.name: Four of a Kind
scoring.description: At least four dice showing the same number

scoring.name: Full House
scoring.description: Three of one number AND two of another number

scoring.name: Yahtzee
scoring.description: All five dice showing the same number
scoring.points: 50 points (first); 100-point bonus for each additional Yahtzee
scoring.example: Roll: 6,6,6,6,6 έΗΤ Score: 50 (or 100 bonus if already scored)

scoring.name: Chance
scoring.example: Roll: 1,2,3,4,5 έΗΤ Score: 15. Use this as a dump for bad rolls.

variations.name: Double Yahtzee Bonus
variations.description: Some house rules award 200 points (instead of 100) for each Yahtzee after the first.

variations.name: Forced Joker Rule
variations.description: If you roll a Yahtzee but already scored 50 in the Yahtzee box, you can use it as a joker in the Upper Section or Lower Section, scoring it normally.

variations.name: Triple Yahtzee
variations.description: Play three rounds simultaneously using three separate scorecards. Scores from column 2 are doubled, column 3 are tripled.

variations.name: Painted Dice
variations.description: Replace standard dice with colored dice έΑΦ each color has unique rules. A fun variant for experienced players.

sampleTurn.description: Aiming for Yahtzee (five of a kind). Player starts with a lucky opening roll.

rolls.note: Three 5s already! Keep them all, re-roll the 3 and 2.
Four 5s! Keep all four, re-roll the 1 for one last shot at Yahtzee.
YAHTZEE! All five dice show 5. Score 50 points in the Yahtzee box!

games.name: Texas Hold'em Poker
games.objective: Win chips by making the best 5-card poker hand using any combination of your 2 hole cards and 5 community cards, or by forcing all opponents to fold.

setup.title: Set up the table
setup.description: Use a standard 52-card deck. Each player starts with an equal number of chips. Assign a dealer button (a small disc or chip marker).

setup.title: Post the blinds
setup.description: The player to the left of the dealer posts the Small Blind (usually half the minimum bet). The player to their left posts the Big Blind (the minimum bet). Blinds start the pot and create action.

setup.title: Deal hole cards
setup.description: The dealer deals 2 cards face-down to each player, starting from the small blind going clockwise. These private cards are your 'hole cards'έΑΦnever show them to opponents.

gameplay.title: Pre-Flop betting
gameplay.description: Starting with the player left of the big blind, each player can: CALL (match the big blind), RAISE (increase the bet), or FOLD (discard cards). The small and big blind also get a chance to act.

gameplay.title: The Flop
gameplay.description: The dealer burns one card (discards face-down) then deals 3 community cards face-up in the center of the table. These are shared by all players. A new betting round begins from the first active player left of the dealer.

gameplay.title: The Turn
gameplay.description: The dealer burns another card and deals 1 more community card face-up (the 4th community card). Another betting round follows the same pattern.

gameplay.title: The River
gameplay.description: The dealer burns a final card and deals the 5th and last community card face-up. The final betting round takes place.

gameplay.title: The Showdown
gameplay.description: If two or more players remain after the final betting round, they reveal their hole cards. The player who makes the best 5-card hand using any combination of their 2 hole cards and the 5 community cards wins the pot.

gameplay.title: Next hand
gameplay.description: The dealer button moves one position clockwise. New blinds are posted and a new hand begins. Continue until agreed stopping time or one player has all the chips.

handRankings.name: Royal Flush
handRankings.description: The highest possible hand έΑΦ five highest cards of the same suit

handRankings.name: Straight Flush
handRankings.description: Five consecutive cards of the same suit

handRankings.name: Four of a Kind
handRankings.description: Four cards of the same rank

handRankings.name: Full House
handRankings.description: Three of a kind plus a pair

handRankings.name: Flush
handRankings.description: Any five cards of the same suit (not consecutive)

handRankings.name: Straight
handRankings.description: Five consecutive cards of any suits

handRankings.name: Three of a Kind
handRankings.description: Three cards of the same rank

handRankings.name: One Pair
handRankings.description: Two cards of the same rank

handRankings.name: High Card
handRankings.description: No combination έΑΦ highest card plays

bettingRounds.name: Pre-Flop
bettingRounds.description: First round of betting. Action starts left of the big blind. Players must at least call the big blind to stay in the hand.

bettingRounds.name: Flop
bettingRounds.description: Second round. First 3 shared cards are dealt. Action starts from the first active player left of the dealer button.

bettingRounds.name: Turn
bettingRounds.description: Third round. One more shared card. Bets often double in limit games. Players narrow down their hand possibilities.

bettingRounds.name: River
bettingRounds.description: Final round. Last shared card is dealt. This is the last chance to bet, bluff, or extract value before showdown.

variations.name: No-Limit Texas Hold'em
variations.description: Players can bet any amount up to all their chips at any time ('all-in'). This is the most popular variant, used in the World Series of Poker Main Event.

variations.name: Pot-Limit Hold'em
variations.description: Players can only bet up to the current size of the pot. Builds more slowly than No-Limit but still allows large bets.

variations.name: Limit Hold'em
variations.description: Bets and raises are fixed amounts. Pre-flop and flop bets equal the big blind; turn and river bets are doubled.

variations.name: Short Deck Hold'em
variations.description: Played with a 36-card deck (2s-5s removed). Hand rankings shift έΑΦ a flush beats a full house.

sampleHand.description: A classic strong hand scenario showing how community cards change the game.

action.description: You're dealt Aέβι Kέβι (Big Slick). You raise 3x the big blind. One opponent calls.
You flop three Kings! You have trip Kings. You bet half the pot to extract value. Opponent calls.
The 2έβο doesn't help your opponent. You bet again έΑΦ building the pot with your strong hand. Opponent raises! You call.
The Ace gives you a Full House: Kings full of Aces (K-K-K-A-A). You move all-in. Opponent calls with Kέβμ 7έβο (full house K-K-K-7-7). Your Aces beat their Sevens!

sampleHand.result: You win with Kings full of Aces (Kέβι Kέβο Kέβξ Aέβι Aέβο)
